questions.text: A charge 1e accelerated through a potential difference of 1000 V acquires K.E _
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: When a charge of 1e (where e is the elementary charge) is accelerated through a potential difference of 1000 V, it acquires kinetic energy. The kinetic energy (K.E) acquired by the charge can be calculated using the formula:K.E=e×V
Incorrect asper calculationK.E=e×V
Incorrect asper calculationK.E=e×V
Incorrect asper calculationK.E=e×V

questions.explanation: When a charge of 1e (where e is the elementary charge) is accelerated through a potential difference of 1000 V, it acquires kinetic energy. The kinetic energy (K.E) acquired by the charge can be calculated using the formula:K.E=e×V

questions.text: A wire of uniform cross-section area A and length L and resistance R is cut into two equal parts. The specific resistance of each part will be _
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Incorrect asper fact and given condition
Incorrect asper fact and given condition
Incorrect asper fact and given condition

When a wire of uniform cross-sectional area A and length L with resistance R is cut into two equal parts, each part will have half the length and half the resistance.The specific resistance (or resistivity) of each part will remain the same because it depends on the material of the wire, not its dimensions. Therefore, the specific resistance of each part will be the same as the original wire.

questions.explanation: When a wire of uniform cross-sectional area A and length L with resistance R is cut into two equal parts, each part will have half the length and half the resistance.The specific resistance (or resistivity) of each part will remain the same because it depends on the material of the wire, not its dimensions. Therefore, the specific resistance of each part will be the same as the original wire.

questions.text: The value of alternating voltage is V=10 Sin2πt. The effective value will be _.
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Incorrect as per calculation

The effective (RMS) value of an alternating voltage \( V = 10 \sin(2\pi t) \) is \( \frac{V_{max}}{\sqrt{2}} \), where \( V_{max} \) is the maximum voltage amplitude. In this case, \( V_{max} = 10 \) volts. So, the effective value is \( \frac{10}{\sqrt{2}} \) volts.
Incorrect as per calculation
Incorrect as per calculation

questions.explanation: The effective (RMS) value of an alternating voltage \( V = 10 \sin(2\pi t) \) is \( \frac{V_{max}}{\sqrt{2}} \), where \( V_{max} \) is the maximum voltage amplitude. In this case, \( V_{max} = 10 \) volts. So, the effective value is \( \frac{10}{\sqrt{2}} \) volts.

questions.text: A small piece of an un-magnetized material gets repelled when it is brought near a powerful magnet. The material is _
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Materials with unpaired electrons aligning with an external magnetic field, weakly attracting it.
The material is likely diamagnetic.Diamagnetic materials are those that create a weak, opposing magnetic field when exposed to an external magnetic field, causing them to be repelled by magnets.
Materials where unpaired electron spins align spontaneously, creating a strong, permanent magnetic field.
Materials that do not exhibit magnetic properties, either inherently or after exposure to a magnetic field.

questions.explanation: The material is likely diamagnetic.Diamagnetic materials are those that create a weak, opposing magnetic field when exposed to an external magnetic field, causing them to be repelled by magnets.

questions.explanation: Copper is not ferromagnetic in nature

questions.text: The Balmer series converges at the shortest wavelength equal to _
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Incorrect as per fact and calculation1/𝜆=𝑅𝐻(1/22−1/𝑛2)
Incorrect as per fact and calculation1/𝜆=𝑅𝐻(1/22−1/𝑛2)

The wavelength of the shortest line in the Balmer series can be calculated using the Rydberg formula:1/𝜆=𝑅𝐻(1/22−1/𝑛2)Therefore, the wavelength of the shortest line in the Balmer series is given by:𝜆min=4/𝑅𝐻
Incorrect as per fact and calculation1/𝜆=𝑅𝐻(1/22−1/𝑛2)

questions.explanation: The wavelength of the shortest line in the Balmer series can be calculated using the Rydberg formula:1/𝜆=𝑅𝐻(1/22−1/𝑛2)Therefore, the wavelength of the shortest line in the Balmer series is given by:𝜆min=4/𝑅𝐻

All above mentioned options use the technology of laser

questions.explanation: All above mentioned options use the technology of laser
